article.description: — DeMar DeRozan scored nine of Sacramento’s 11 points in the second overtime, and the Kings beat the Miami Heat 123-118 on Monday night to extend their winning streak to a season-high five games under interim coach Doug Christie.
article.headline: DeRozan shines as Kings hold off Heat 123-118 in double overtime

images.caption: DeMar DeRozan drains a pair of nice shots for the Kings late in the second overtime en route to a victory over the Heat.
images.name: DeMar DeRozan's late heroics help propel Kings to 2OT win

article.linkText: DeRozan shines as Kings hold off Heat 123-118 in double overtime
article.story: SACRAMENTO, Calif. -- — DeMar DeRozan scored nine of Sacramento’s 11 points in the second overtime, and the Kings beat the Miami Heat 123-118 on Monday night to extend their winning streak to a season-high five games under interim coach Doug Christie. DeRozan finished with 30 points on 12-of-26 shooting. Domantas Sabonis had 21 points, 18 rebounds and 11 assists as the Kings (18-19) won their second straight without injured point guard De’Aaron Fox. Malik Monk scored 23 points, Keon Ellis had 17 and Keegan Murray added 14 points and 12 rebounds. Jaime Jaquez Jr. had a triple-double with 16 points, 12 rebounds and 10 assists for Miami. Tyler Herro scored 26 but was called for a costly 8-second violation toward the end of the fourth quarter. Terry Rozier added 18 points, and Haywood Highsmith had 14. The game was tied at 102 at the end of regulation and at 112 after the first overtime. DeRozan took over after that with a 3-pointer and a trio of step-back jumpers that stunned Miami and left the Heat winless in three overtime games on the road this season. Takeaways Heat: Coach Erik Spoelstra’s team played solid for the first four quarters but got sloppy in overtime. Kings: Sacramento is playing with a lot more heart and effort under Christie than it did the final two weeks under former coach Mike Brown. Key moment With his team clinging to a one-point lead in the first overtime, Jaquez missed a pair of free throws with 3 seconds left. That opened the door for the Kings to score and force the second OT. Key stat The Heat are 4-7 without Jimmy Butler this season. Up next Heat: Face the Golden State Warriors on Tuesday. Kings: Begin a four-game trip against the Boston Celtics on Friday. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/nba

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.

images.caption: The Celtics' title odds have shortened since Jayson Tatum's return last week.

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Even after a rough stretch of games, the Thunder remain the heavy favorites to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Adding James Harden to a talented roster with the likes of Donovan Mitchell gives the Cavs a higher upside this season.
images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.
images.name: Jaylen Brown [1296x729]

images.alt: Jamal Murray #27 of the Denver Nuggets dribbles the ball during the game against the Dallas Mavericks on January 14, 2026 at American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas.
images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.
